description: Generic goal-shape categories. Entries may name tactic categories, but do not store per-lemma tactic sequences, goal-state chains, or examples.
how_to_use: Match the current goal to a structural category, then derive concrete tactics from the live goal state.

categories.description: Two-sided equiv (Pr[G1]=Pr[G2]) where after proc;inline*, programs have aligned structure but sampling order differs, or one side has extra masking (OTP). Needs swap to align, then rnd bijection.
indicators: One side has extra multiplication/XOR on a sampled value
typical_tactics: rnd (fun x => x + mask) (fun x => x - mask)

categories.description: Two-sided equiv where programs are structurally identical or nearly so. sim or call+sim closes it.
indicators: Left and right programs have same structure

categories.description: Two-sided equiv where programs have a while loop and coupling is done inside the loop body via rnd bijection (e.g., bit flip).
indicators: rnd with conditional bijection inside loop body
typical_tactics: wp; rnd (fun b => if condition then !b else b)

categories.description: Prove Pr[game]=1/2 via byphoare. The game returns b'=b where b is uniform and independent of adversary. Use swap to move b to end, rnd (pred1 b'), then show all prior steps are lossless.
indicators: Program has b <$ {0,1} and b' <@ A.guess(...)

categories.description: Prove a probability or hoare property about a program with a while loop. Need loop invariant + variant for termination.
indicators: Need to track loop counter and accumulated state

categories.description: Prove islossless (procedure terminates with probability 1). Chain of rnd predT for each sampling, call _ll for each oracle call, working backward.
Combine previously proved lemma bounds via rewrite chain. Typically the final theorem in a security proof.
Game-hopping proof: introduce each intermediate bound with have, then combine with smt. The proof is a sequence of have h0 := lemma0 &m. have h1 := lemma1 &m. ... smt().
typical_tactics: smt() or by smt(h0 h1 h2).

categories.description: Pure logic/set theory: prove fset equality, membership, or restriction properties. No program, just set rewriting.
indicators: Goal involves fset, is_restr, restr, mem_oflist, mem_iota
typical_tactics: rewrite /is_restr /restr in_fsetI in_fsetU in_fset1 mem_oflist mem_iota

categories.description: Pure algebraic/logic identity: XOR cancellation, parse/format roundtrip, arithmetic. No program.
indicators: Goal is a pure equality or implication

categories.description: Trivial program equivalence: apply an existing equiv lemma directly.
indicators: An equiv lemma for exactly this pair already exists

categories.description: Correctness proof: show decrypt(encrypt(m))=m. Typically proc; inline *; auto closes it, possibly with ring/algebra for group arithmetic.
typical_tactics: possibly followed by ring or algebra for group exponent simplification

categories.description: Prove distribution uniformity via bypr: reduce equiv to showing Pr[event=a] is the same for all a. Case split on a (true/false for bool), use byequiv(sim) per case.
indicators: Need to show same distribution for different inputs

categories.description: Apply an abstract lemma from a cloned theory (e.g., DLog.dlog_standard_reduction, Ad1.pr_abs). The proof is essentially instantiating the abstract result.
indicators: Goal matches an abstract lemma signature
Proof is apply or have := lemma

categories.description: Prove Pr[game]=1 (completeness). The game always succeeds. Use byphoare with algebraic verification of the success condition.
Prove extraction from two accepting transcripts (sigma protocol special soundness). Use byphoare, then algebraic extraction of witness via discrete log.
indicators: Extract witness via division in exponent
typical_tactics: algebra or loge + mulrK for discrete log extraction

categories.description: Prove a bound using bad event abstraction (Lemma test/test1 pattern). Apply abstract test lemma, then discharge sub-goals about negated adversary and probability complement.
Prove equiv via transitivity: show G1 ~ G_mid and G_mid ~ G2. Used when direct equiv is hard due to structural mismatch.
